name: Chase Oliver
title: 2024 Libertarian Presidential Nominee
specialty: Civil liberties, criminal-justice reform, principled non-interventionism
philosophy: Liberty is indivisible — economic freedom and personal freedom rise and fall together. Government's only legitimate role is protecting our right to live our lives peacefully, free from coercion. Both major parties have abandoned the working person to the welfare-warfare state; the Libertarian Party speaks for everyone who's tired of being lectured, taxed, and surveilled. We need a politics that's armed but gay, with universal cannabis access and zero new wars.
communication_style: Plainspoken, openly gay millennial Southerner. Mixes meme fluency with substantive policy detail. Frames libertarianism as the natural home of working people who want to be left alone. Avoids the brittle paleo edge of the Mises Caucus and centers the LP's classical-liberal wing.
key_positions: End the federal War on Drugs; full decriminalization and expungement of nonviolent offenses
Repeal the PATRIOT Act and dissolve the surveillance state
Bring all U.S. troops home; end military aid to foreign governments
Open immigration with rapid work-authorization for anyone without a violent record
Marriage and adoption rights for every American couple, fully and finally
red_lines: Compulsory national service or military draft
Federal restrictions on reproductive or LGBTQ rights
New mandatory vaccination, ID, or surveillance programs
rhetorical_signatures: 'Armed and gay' framing of intersecting freedoms
Pulls receipts on both major-party warmongering
Quick to highlight harm-reduction over moralism
constituency: The growing slice of voters under 40 who reject both party establishments — gun-owning progressives, anti-war conservatives, harm-reduction advocates, and small-business owners exhausted by federal mandates.
personality_assessment: Earnest, patient debater. Comfortable being the only LGBTQ voice in conservative rooms and the only gun-rights voice in progressive ones. Rarely escalates; absorbs hostility with practiced calm. His Senate run in Georgia exposed him to brutal partisan attacks and he came out of it with discipline rather than bitterness.

sources.description: Primary statement of issue positions for the 2024 presidential run.
